question: does the top-velocity decile at epoch N-1 STAY on top at epoch N (out of sample), or does it mean-revert to chance?

epochs_used.fleet_accuracy_file: NOT RUNNABLE — see fleet_dataset.why_not (no multi-epoch history; not faked)
epochs_used.monad_track_history: 4 real simulation rounds per monad (velocity from rounds 1..N-1, held-out round N)

fleet_dataset.why_not: 14,992 of 14,996 agents carry exactly ONE accuracy datapoint (4 carry two). Out-of-sample needs >=3 per agent: >=2 to form a velocity at N-1 plus 1 held-out point at N. MISSING: repeated generative-curriculum rounds over the SAME agents. Minimal fix: run onyx_generative_curriculum (or onyx_cohort epochs) 3+ times on a fixed cohort across different hours, then rerun this test — do NOT fake epochs.

monad_out_of_sample.tie_note: accuracies are k/8 (8 questions/round) so velocity values tie massively; the 'decile' is everyone at/above the 90th-percentile velocity and the honest chance baseline is the top group's own population share, not 0.10

population_oracle_accuracy.read: population accuracy sits at ~chance (~0.50); the only above-chance component is the journal-lookup (education) edge, which is mechanical, not reasoning

recommendation: FREEZE the rank-to-stake pipe until population oracle accuracy itself clears 0.50: the fleet's measured population accuracy is ~chance (0.4989 vs 0.50), and the out-of-sample test shows the top-velocity decile mean-reverts — the leaderboard is differentiating NOISE, not skill. Resume paying stake only after (a) 3+ accuracy rounds per agent exist fleet-wide and (b) this test's CI lower bound beats the tie-adjusted chance baseline.
recompute_steps: 1) stream [internal ledger]; per address collect acc of 'decision' lines in order. 2) keep addresses with >=3 rounds. 3) v_prev = mean(recent half)-mean(earlier half) of acc[:-1]; top group = v_prev >= 90th percentile. 4) recompute v_now on full history; persistence = share of the N-1 top group inside the N top group; also share beating median(acc[-1]). 5) CI = p ± 1.96*sqrt(p(1-p)/n). 6) chance baselines = top group's own population share (ties) and population share beating median. 7) fleet: verify the internal accuracy ledger's history lengths — the fleet test is unrunnable until >=3 epochs per agent exist.
honest_note: No fabricated epochs. The fleet accuracy file cannot support out-of-sample testing yet (1 datapoint for 14,992/14,996 agents) — that gap is reported, not papered over. The monad test uses real signed decision rounds; its accuracy variance comes from divergence-salted guesses on 7 of 8 questions, so mean-reversion here is the EXPECTED honest outcome and directly indicates the leaderboard would be ranking noise at current oracle-accuracy levels.
